title: A 2-Foot Gator Named Irwin Escaped an Indiana School Show
script: A gator's on the loose! Mark Kohlhorst runs Mark's Ark, an animal show that performs at Indiana schools. On June 9 in Auburn, he returned home from a show and found the cooler empty — his 2-foot American alligator, Irwin, was gone. Kohlhorst has reported previous escapes from his property: a pony, a tortoise, a pig, and an emu have all wandered off before. Indiana law allows anyone to keep an alligator under 5 feet without a permit. Authorities in DeKalb County launched a search but Irwin remained at large. Neighbors told NewsNation the property has a long history of exotic animals turning up in the neighborhood. Kohlhorst says Irwin poses no danger to humans. Has DeKalb County found Irwin yet, or is he planning his own backyard barbecue?
